1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# scripts/benchmark/benchmark — operator-facing benchmark driver.
3	#
4	# Replaces `fiz-bench sweep` / `fiz-bench matrix` execution. Pure shell:
5	# yq + jq + sha256sum + docker. No Go bridge, no Python.
6	#
7	# Bead fizeau-fe87a3ac: skeleton + listing subcommands + --plan mode +
8	# preflight rebuild gate. The cell execution loop (resume, retry, signal
9	# handling, concurrency, budget) lands in a follow-up bead.
10	#
11	# Operator contract:
12	#   ./benchmark profiles
13	#   ./benchmark bench-sets
14	#   ./benchmark harness-adapters
15	#   ./benchmark task-executors
16	#   ./benchmark validate                            # placeholder; see PR C
17	#   ./benchmark preflight                           # rebuild harbor-runner if sha drifted
18	#   ./benchmark --profile P[,P2,...] --bench-set B[,B2,...] --plan
19	#   ./benchmark --profile P[,P2,...] --bench-set B[,B2,...]   # run path; PR A4
20	#
21	# Rules:
22	#   1. --plan is pure: no file writes, no Docker pulls/builds, no
23	#      benchmark-results/ creation. Prints one matrix line per cell.
24	#   2. --profile and --bench-set are both required for --plan and run.
25	#   3. preflight is the only command that touches Docker.
26

57	die() { printf 'benchmark: %s\n' "$*" >&2; exit 1; }
58	warn() { printf 'benchmark: %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

60	require_tool() {
61	  command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1 || die "required tool not found: $1"
62	}

64	# in_flight_state_dir returns the host-specific state directory.
65	in_flight_state_dir() {
66	  local hostname
67	  hostname="$(hostname)"
68	  printf '%s/%s\n' "${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR}" "${hostname}"
69	}
70	
71	# in_flight_lock_path returns the lock path for a concurrency group.
72	in_flight_lock_path() {
73	  local group="$1"
74	  printf '%s/locks/%s.lock\n' "${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR}" "${group}"
75	}
76	
77	# in_flight_json_path returns the path to the in-flight state JSON file.
78	in_flight_json_path() {
79	  printf '%s/in-flight.json\n' "$(in_flight_state_dir)"
80	}
81	
82	# acquire_group_lock acquires an flock for a concurrency group, blocking if needed.
83	# Returns 0 on success, prints the lock fd to stdout.
84	acquire_group_lock() {
85	  local group="$1"
86	  local lock_path
87	  lock_path="$(in_flight_lock_path "${group}")"
88	  mkdir -p "$(dirname "${lock_path}")"
89	  local lock_fd
90	  lock_fd=200
91	  eval "exec ${lock_fd}>'${lock_path}'"
92	  flock "${lock_fd}" || die "failed to acquire lock for concurrency-group=${group}"
93	  printf '%d\n' "${lock_fd}"
94	}
95	
96	# release_group_lock releases an flock acquired by acquire_group_lock.
97	release_group_lock() {
98	  local lock_fd="$1"
99	  eval "exec ${lock_fd}>&-" || true
100	}

137	# launch_cell_worker starts a cell in a fresh session so the worker's PID
138	# doubles as the process-group leader for interrupt reaping.
139	launch_cell_worker() {
140	  local lock_fd="$1"
141	  shift
142	  setsid bash -c '
143	    budget_or_run_cell "$@"
144	    status=$?
145	    release_group_lock "${!#}"
146	    exit "${status}"
147	  ' bash "$@" "${lock_fd}" &
148	}

150	# register_inflight registers a cell as in-flight in the state file.
151	# Reads current entries, adds this cell, removes dead PIDs, writes back.
152	register_inflight() {
153	  local cell_id="$1" cell_dir="$2"
154	  local state_dir
155	  state_dir="$(in_flight_state_dir)"
156	  mkdir -p "${state_dir}"
157	  local json_path
158	  json_path="$(in_flight_json_path)"
159	
160	  local current_json="{\"cells\":[]}"
161	  [[ -f "${json_path}" ]] && current_json="$(cat "${json_path}")" || true
162	
163	  # Prune dead PIDs by filtering in bash
164	  local live_cells=()
165	  local cell_json pid
166	  while IFS= read -r cell_json; do
167	    [[ -z "${cell_json}" ]] && continue
168	    pid="$(jq -r '.pid' <<<"${cell_json}")"
169	    if [[ -n "${pid}" ]] && kill -0 "${pid}" 2>/dev/null; then
170	      live_cells+=("${cell_json}")
171	    fi
172	  done < <(jq -r '.cells[] | @json' <<<"${current_json}")
173	
174	  # Rebuild with live cells
175	  current_json="$(printf '%s\n' "${live_cells[@]}" | jq -s '{cells:.}')"
176	
177	  # Add this cell
178	  current_json="$(jq \
179	    --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
180	    --arg cell_dir "${cell_dir}" \
181	    --argjson pid "${BASHPID}" \
182	    '.cells += [{cell_id:$cell_id, cell_dir:$cell_dir, pid:$pid}]' \
183	    <<<"${current_json}")"
184	
185	  printf '%s\n' "${current_json}" >"${json_path}.tmp"
186	  mv "${json_path}.tmp" "${json_path}"
187	}

190	# unregister_inflight removes a cell from the in-flight state file.
191	unregister_inflight() {
192	  local cell_id="$1"
193	  local json_path
194	  json_path="$(in_flight_json_path)"
195	  [[ -f "${json_path}" ]] || return 0
196	
197	  local updated
198	  updated="$(jq \
199	    --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
200	    '.cells |= map(select(.cell_id != $cell_id))' \
201	    "${json_path}")"
202	
203	  printf '%s\n' "${updated}" >"${json_path}.tmp"
204	  mv "${json_path}.tmp" "${json_path}"
205	}

207	# interrupt_inflight_cells is called during signal handling to mark in-flight
208	# cells as interrupted and clean them up from the JSON file.
209	interrupt_inflight_cells() {
210	  local json_path
211	  json_path="$(in_flight_json_path)"
212	  [[ -f "${json_path}" ]] || return 0
213	
214	  local cells
215	  cells="$(jq -r '.cells[] | @json' "${json_path}")" || return 0
216	
217	  local cell_id cell_dir pid
218	  while IFS= read -r cell_json; do
219	    [[ -z "${cell_json}" ]] && continue
220	    cell_id="$(jq -r '.cell_id' <<<"${cell_json}")"
221	    cell_dir="$(jq -r '.cell_dir' <<<"${cell_json}")"
222	    pid="$(jq -r '.pid' <<<"${cell_json}")"
223	
224	    # Try to kill the cell's process group
225	    if [[ -n "${pid}" ]] && (( pid > 0 )); then
226	      kill -TERM -"${pid}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || kill -TERM "${pid}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
227	    fi
228	
229	    # Try to docker stop any harbor-runner containers in this cell
230	    if [[ -d "${cell_dir}" ]]; then
231	      # The container ID is typically embedded in result.json or we can search by cell_dir
232	      # For now, we attempt a generic docker stop on containers that might be for this cell
233	      docker ps -q --filter "label=fizeau-cell=${cell_id}" 2>/dev/null \
234	        | xargs -r docker stop >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
235	    fi
236	  done <<<"${cells}"
237	}

306	# list_adapter_dir prints one "<name>\t<summary>" line per executable file.
307	# Files without a `# SUMMARY:` line on line 2 print "(no summary)" so the
308	# omission is visible. CONTRACT.md and other non-executables are skipped.
309	list_adapter_dir() {
310	  local dir="$1"
311	  [[ -d "${dir}" ]] || { warn "directory missing: ${dir}"; return 0; }
312	  local f name summary
313	  local rows=()
314	  shopt -s nullglob dotglob
315	  for f in "${dir}"/*; do
316	    [[ -f "${f}" && -x "${f}" ]] || continue
317	    name="$(basename "${f}")"
318	    summary="$(sed -n '2{/^# SUMMARY:[[:space:]]*/{s///;p;};q;}' "${f}")"
319	    [[ -n "${summary}" ]] || summary="(no summary)"
320	    rows+=("$(printf '%s\t%s' "${name}" "${summary}")")
321	  done
322	  shopt -u nullglob dotglob
323	  if [[ ${#rows[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
324	    warn "no adapters found in ${dir}"
325	    return 0
326	  fi
327	  printf '%s\n' "${rows[@]}" | LC_ALL=C sort -u
328	}

354	bench_set_json() {

359	# resolve_tasks emits one task id per line for a bench-set.
360	# Handles three shapes:
361	#   tasks: [id, id, ...]         inline list
362	#   tasks: all                   defer to harness dataset; emit "*all*"
363	#   tasks_from: <path>           load tasks[].id from a sibling YAML
364	# tasks_from and inline tasks merge in deterministic order with duplicates
365	# removed on first occurrence.
366	resolve_tasks() {
367	  local bs_file="$1"
368	  local bs_json tasks_kind tasks_from
369	  bs_json="$(bench_set_json "${bs_file}")"
370	
371	  tasks_kind="$(jq -r '
372	    if (.tasks // null) == null then "none"
373	    elif (.tasks | type) == "array" then "list"
374	    elif (.tasks | type) == "string" then ("string:" + .tasks)
375	    else "other"
376	    end' <<<"${bs_json}")"
377	
378	  tasks_from="$(jq -r '.tasks_from // ""' <<<"${bs_json}")"
379	
380	  if [[ "${tasks_kind}" == "string:all" ]]; then
381	    [[ -z "${tasks_from}" ]] || die "bench-set ${bs_file}: tasks: all cannot be combined with tasks_from"
382	    # Defer to the harness dataset. The plan path emits a sentinel so
383	    # operators see the contract; the run path (PR A4) expands at execution.
384	    printf '*all*\n'
385	    return 0
386	  fi
387	
388	  local -A seen_tasks=()
389	  local emitted=0
390	  local task resolved
391	
392	  emit_task() {
393	    local task="$1"
394	    [[ -n "${task}" ]] || return 0
395	    if [[ -z "${seen_tasks["${task}"]+x}" ]]; then
396	      seen_tasks["${task}"]=1
397	      printf '%s\n' "${task}"
398	      emitted=1
399	    fi
400	  }
401	
402	  if [[ -n "${tasks_from}" ]]; then
403	    resolved="$(cd "$(dirname "${bs_file}")" && cd "$(dirname "${tasks_from}")" && pwd)/$(basename "${tasks_from}")"
404	    [[ -f "${resolved}" ]] || die "tasks_from path not found: ${tasks_from} (resolved to ${resolved})"
405	    while IFS= read -r task; do
406	      emit_task "${task}"
407	    done < <(
408	      yq -o=json '.' "${resolved}" \
409	        | jq -r '
410	            .tasks[]?
411	            | (if type == "object" then (.id // empty) else . end)
412	          '
413	    )
414	  fi
415	
416	  if [[ "${tasks_kind}" == "list" ]]; then
417	    while IFS= read -r task; do
418	      emit_task "${task}"
419	    done < <(jq -r '
420	      .tasks[] | (if type == "object" then (.id // empty) else . end)
421	    ' <<<"${bs_json}")
422	  elif [[ "${tasks_kind}" != "none" ]]; then
423	    die "bench-set ${bs_file}: unsupported tasks shape"
424	  fi
425	
426	  if [[ ${emitted} -eq 0 ]]; then
427	    if [[ -n "${tasks_from}" || "${tasks_kind}" == "list" ]]; then
428	      die "bench-set ${bs_file}: resolved no tasks"
429	    fi
430	    die "bench-set ${bs_file}: no .tasks list, .tasks: all, or .tasks_from set"
431	  fi
432	}

531	  [[ ${#files[@]} -gt 0 ]] || die "validate: no profiles found in ${PROFILES_DIR}"
532

535	    json="$(yq -o=json '.' "${f}")"
536	    profile_id="$(jq -r '.id // ""' <<<"${json}")"
537	    [[ -n "${profile_id}" ]] || die "validate: profile ${name} is missing .id"

539	
540	    concurrency_group="$(jq -r '.concurrency_group // ""' <<<"${json}")"
541	    [[ -n "${concurrency_group}" ]] || die "validate: profile ${name} is missing .concurrency_group"
542	    jq -e --arg group "${concurrency_group}" '.groups[$group] != null' <<<"${concurrency_json}" >/dev/null \
543	      || die "validate: profile ${name} references unknown concurrency_group ${concurrency_group}"

545	    harness="$(jq -r '.harness // "none"' <<<"${json}")"

556	  local f json name bench_set_id framework dataset reps task seen_task
557	  local files=()

560	    files+=("${f}")
561	  done

564

567	    json="$(bench_set_json "${f}")"

569	    [[ -n "${bench_set_id}" ]] || die "validate: bench-set ${name} is missing .id"
570	    [[ "${bench_set_id}" == "${name}" ]] || die "validate: bench-set ${name} id mismatch (${bench_set_id})"
571	
572	    framework="$(jq -r '.framework // ""' <<<"${json}")"

596	  local tmpdir="$1" synthetic_profile="$2"
597	  local artifact="${tmpdir}/agent-artifact"
598	  : >"${artifact}"
599	
600	  local f name install_out command_out install_err command_err install_status command_status
601	  local files=()
602	  shopt -s nullglob dotglob
603	  for f in "${HARNESS_ADAPTERS_DIR}"/*; do

613	    command_err="${tmpdir}/${name}.command.err"

615	    set +e

617	    install_status=$?

619	    if [[ ${install_status} -ne 0 ]]; then

621	    fi
622	    jq -e '
623	      type == "object"

625	      and (.artifact_source | type == "string" and length > 0)
626	      and (.binary_path | type == "string")
627	      and (has("harbor_plugin"))

637	    fi

639	      type == "object"

641	      and all(.command[]?; type == "string")
642	      and (.env | type == "object")
643	      and all((.env // {}) | to_entries[]?; (.value | type == "string"))
644	      and (.secret_env_keys | type == "array")
645	      and ((.secret_env_keys | unique | length) == (.secret_env_keys | length))
646	      and (.env as $env | all(.secret_env_keys[]?; $env[.] != null))
647	    ' <<<"${command_out}" >/dev/null \
648	      || die "validate: harness-adapter ${name} command output is malformed"

697	  IFS=',' read -r -a profile_ids <<<"${profiles_csv}"
698	  IFS=',' read -r -a bench_set_ids <<<"${bench_sets_csv}"

701	  local rep i n

703	  for profile_id in "${profile_ids[@]}"; do
704	    [[ -n "${profile_id}" ]] || continue
705	    resolve_profile_file "${profile_id}" >/dev/null  # existence check

735	# preflight: rebuild harbor-runner if image-content-sha drifted
736	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
737	
738	# compute_harbor_source_sha mirrors build-harbor-runner.sh's algorithm: a
739	# sha256 over harbor_adapters/* + harbor_agent.py + Dockerfile.harbor-runner.
740	# Kept in sync by re-computing the same inputs; the two scripts MUST agree.
741	compute_harbor_source_sha() {
742	  local adapters_dir="${SCRIPT_DIR}/harbor_adapters"
743	  local agent_file="${SCRIPT_DIR}/harbor_agent.py"
744	  local dockerfile="${SCRIPT_DIR}/Dockerfile.harbor-runner"
745	  [[ -d "${adapters_dir}" ]] || die "preflight: harbor_adapters/ missing (${adapters_dir})"
746	  [[ -f "${agent_file}" ]] || die "preflight: harbor_agent.py missing (${agent_file})"
747	  [[ -f "${dockerfile}" ]] || die "preflight: Dockerfile.harbor-runner missing (${dockerfile})"
748	  {
749	    LC_ALL=C find "${adapters_dir}" -type f \
750	      | LC_ALL=C sort \
751	      | while IFS= read -r f; do
752	          printf '%s  %s\n' "$(sha256sum "${f}" | awk '{print $1}')" "${f#"${SCRIPT_DIR}/"}"
753	        done
754	    printf '%s  %s\n' "$(sha256sum "${agent_file}" | awk '{print $1}')" "harbor_agent.py"
755	    printf '%s  %s\n' "$(sha256sum "${dockerfile}" | awk '{print $1}')" "Dockerfile.harbor-runner"
756	  } | sha256sum | awk '{print $1}'
757	}

759	# cached_harbor_image_sha prints the image-content-sha label of the cached

763	  command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1 || { printf ''; return 0; }
764	  docker image inspect --format '{{ index .Config.Labels "image-content-sha" }}' "${tag}" 2>/dev/null || printf ''

767	cmd_preflight() {
768	  require_tool sha256sum
769	
770	  local failed=0
771	  local source_sha cached_sha
772	  local state_dir host_state_dir lock_dir probe
773	  local build_output build_rc
774	
775	  printf 'preflight checklist:\n'
776	
777	  check_tool() {
778	    local tool="$1"
779	    if command -v "${tool}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
780	      printf '  [x] %s (%s)\n' "${tool}" "$(command -v "${tool}")"
781	      return 0
782	    fi
783	    printf '  [ ] %s (missing)\n' "${tool}"
784	    failed=1
785	    return 1
786	  }
787	
788	  check_writable_dir() {
789	    local label="$1" dir="$2" tmp
790	    if mkdir -p "${dir}" 2>/dev/null; then
791	      tmp="${dir}/.preflight.$$"
792	      if : >"${tmp}" 2>/dev/null; then
793	        rm -f "${tmp}"
794	        printf '  [x] %s (%s)\n' "${label}" "${dir}"
795	        return 0
796	      fi
797	    fi
798	    printf '  [ ] %s (%s not writable)\n' "${label}" "${dir}"
799	    failed=1
800	    return 1
801	  }
802	
803	  check_tool yq
804	  check_tool jq
805	  check_tool docker
806	  check_tool setsid
807	  check_tool flock
808	
809	  state_dir="$(in_flight_state_dir)"
810	  lock_dir="${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR}/locks"
811	  check_writable_dir "benchmark state dir" "${state_dir}"
812	  check_writable_dir "benchmark lock dir" "${lock_dir}"
813	
814	  source_sha="$(compute_harbor_source_sha)"
815	  cached_sha="$(cached_harbor_image_sha "${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}")"
816	  printf '  [x] harbor source fingerprint (%s)\n' "${source_sha}"
817	  printf '  [x] cached harbor image fingerprint (%s)\n' "${cached_sha:-<none>}"
818	
819	  if [[ -n "${cached_sha}" && "${cached_sha}" == "${source_sha}" ]]; then
820	    printf '  [x] harbor-runner image up to date (%s)\n' "${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}"
821	  else
822	    if [[ ! -x "${HARBOR_BUILD_SCRIPT}" ]]; then
823	      printf '  [ ] harbor-runner build script executable (%s missing or not executable)\n' "${HARBOR_BUILD_SCRIPT}"
824	      failed=1
825	    else
826	      printf '  [*] building harbor-runner image via %s\n' "${HARBOR_BUILD_SCRIPT}"
827	      set +e
828	      build_output="$(IMAGE_TAG="${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}" "${HARBOR_BUILD_SCRIPT}" 2>&1)"
829	      build_rc=$?
830	      set -e
831	      printf '%s\n' "${build_output}"
832	      if [[ ${build_rc} -eq 0 ]]; then
833	        printf '  [x] harbor-runner image buildable (%s)\n' "${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}"
834	      else
835	        printf '  [ ] harbor-runner image buildable (%s)\n' "${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}"
836	        failed=1
837	      fi
838	    fi
839	  fi
840	
841	  if [[ ${failed} -ne 0 ]]; then
842	    printf 'preflight: one or more checks failed\n' >&2
843	    return 1
844	  fi
845	
846	  printf 'preflight: all checks passed\n'
847	}

853	# Terminal final_status values that satisfy resume coverage. Non-terminal
854	# statuses (running, transient_exhausted, missing_result) do not count as
855	# coverage; --retry-invalid surfaces invalid_class != "" or orphan state.
856	is_terminal_status() {
857	  case "$1" in
858	    completed|pass|fail|timeout|invalid|completed_no_grade) return 0 ;;
859	    *) return 1 ;;
860	  esac
861	}

863	# Map the profile's `harness:` field to a harness-adapter file name.
864	# `none` (or absent) means native fiz.
865	resolve_adapter_for_harness() {
866	  case "${1:-}" in
867	    ""|none) printf 'fiz\n' ;;
868	    anthropic) printf 'claude\n' ;;
869	    *)       printf '%s\n' "$1" ;;
870	  esac
871	}

873	# Mint a fresh cell-id: UTC timestamp + 4 random hex chars.
874	new_cell_id() {
875	  local ts rand
876	  ts="$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)"
877	  rand="$(LC_ALL=C tr -dc 'a-f0-9' </dev/urandom 2>/dev/null | head -c 4 || true)"
878	  if [[ -z "${rand}" || ${#rand} -lt 4 ]]; then
879	    rand="$(printf '%04x' $((RANDOM & 0xFFFF)))"
880	  fi
881	  printf '%s-%s\n' "${ts}" "${rand}"
882	}

884	# task_executor_version: sha256 of the harbor task-executor script. The
885	# task-executor is shell, so the script bytes are the version of record.
886	task_executor_version() {
887	  local f="${TASK_EXECUTORS_DIR}/harbor"
888	  if [[ -f "${f}" ]]; then
889	    sha256sum "${f}" | awk '{print $1}'
890	  else
891	    printf 'unknown\n'
892	  fi

895	# harbor_runner_image_digest: docker image Id for fizeau-harbor-runner. When
896	# docker is absent (CI / dry-run), fall back to the source-content sha so the
897	# sweep manifest is still well-defined.
898	harbor_runner_image_digest() {
899	  # Test/operator override wins so suites can pin the recorded digest.
900	  if [[ -n "${BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE:-}" ]]; then
901	    printf '%s\n' "${BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE}"
902	    return 0
903	  fi
904	  local tag="${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}"
905	  if command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
906	    local d
907	    d="$(docker image inspect --format '{{.Id}}' "${tag}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
908	    if [[ -n "${d}" ]]; then
909	      printf '%s\n' "${d}"
910	      return 0
911	    fi
912	  fi
913	  # No docker (or image absent). Derive from the source-content sha used by
914	  # preflight.
915	  if compute_harbor_source_sha >/dev/null 2>&1; then
916	    printf 'src:%s\n' "$(compute_harbor_source_sha)"
917	  else
918	    printf 'unknown\n'
919	  fi
920	}

938	# compose_env_redacted prints command-spec env with secret_env_keys replaced
939	# by a redaction sentinel. Order-insensitive; entries missing from env are
940	# ignored.
941	compose_env_redacted() {
942	  local env_json="$1" secret_keys_json="$2"
943	  jq -c --argjson secrets "${secret_keys_json}" '
944	    . as $env
945	    | reduce ($secrets[]? | tostring) as $k
946	        ($env; if has($k) then .[$k] = "***REDACTED***" else . end)
947	  ' <<<"${env_json}"
948	}

950	# is_transient_stderr greps the executor's stderr for known transient error
951	# signatures: connection refused/reset, HTTP 5xx, unexpected/EOF parse.
952	is_transient_stderr() {
953	  local f="$1"
954	  [[ -s "${f}" ]] || return 1
955	  LC_ALL=C grep -qE 'connection refused|connection reset|HTTP 5[0-9][0-9]|status[ =:]?5[0-9][0-9]|status_code=5[0-9][0-9]|unexpected EOF|EOF parse|EOF while|temporarily unavailable' "${f}"
956	}
957	
958	# Invoke the task-executor with bounded exponential backoff on transient
959	# errors. Returns 0 on success; non-zero on terminal failure after retries.
960	# Captures attempts log to ${cell_dir}/retry-log.txt for forensics.
961	run_executor_with_retry() {
962	  local task_spec="$1" cell_dir="$2" executor="$3"
963	  local stderr_file
964	  stderr_file="$(mktemp)"
965	  local max_attempts="${BENCH_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS}"
966	  local backoff="${BENCH_RETRY_BACKOFF_BASE}"
967	  local attempt=1
968	  local status=0
969	  local retry_log="${cell_dir}/retry-log.txt"
970	  : >"${retry_log}"
971	  while :; do
972	    : >"${stderr_file}"
973	    set +e
974	    printf '%s' "${task_spec}" | "${executor}" 2>"${stderr_file}" >/dev/null
975	    status=$?
976	    set -e
977	    printf 'attempt=%d status=%d\n' "${attempt}" "${status}" >>"${retry_log}"
978	    if [[ ${status} -eq 0 ]]; then
979	      rm -f "${stderr_file}"
980	      return 0
981	    fi
982	    cat "${stderr_file}" >>"${retry_log}"
983	    if (( attempt > max_attempts )) || ! is_transient_stderr "${stderr_file}"; then
984	      cat "${stderr_file}" >&2
985	      rm -f "${stderr_file}"
986	      return "${status}"
987	    fi
988	    warn "cell $(basename "${cell_dir}"): transient error (attempt ${attempt}); sleeping ${backoff}s"
989	    sleep "${backoff}"
990	    backoff=$((backoff * 2))
991	    attempt=$((attempt + 1))
992	  done
993	}

1000	    printf '%s\n' "${BENCH_TASKS_DIR}"
1001	    return 0

1003	  case "${framework}" in

1005	    *)              printf '%s\n' "${BENCH_TASKS_DIR_DEFAULT}" ;;
1006	  esac

1009	# Count existing terminal cells matching (profile_id, task) under <out>; also
1010	# emit lists of invalid cells and orphan cells (cells with cell-state.json
1011	# and no terminal report.json). Lines are written to the named output paths.
1012	inspect_existing_cells() {
1013	  local out="$1" dataset_dir="$2" task="$3" profile_id="$4"
1014	  local terminal_out="$5" invalid_out="$6" orphan_out="$7"
1015	  : >"${terminal_out}"
1016	  : >"${invalid_out}"
1017	  : >"${orphan_out}"
1018	  local task_dir="${out}/cells/${dataset_dir}/${task}"
1019	  [[ -d "${task_dir}" ]] || return 0
1020	  local cell
1021	  shopt -s nullglob
1022	  for cell in "${task_dir}"/*/; do
1023	    local cdir="${cell%/}"
1024	    local report="${cdir}/report.json"
1025	    local state="${cdir}/cell-state.json"
1026	    if [[ -f "${report}" ]]; then
1027	      local pid status inv
1028	      pid="$(jq -r '.profile.id // ""' "${report}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
1029	      [[ "${pid}" == "${profile_id}" ]] || continue
1030	      status="$(jq -r '.final_status // ""' "${report}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
1031	      inv="$(jq -r '.invalid_class // ""' "${report}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
1032	      if [[ -n "${inv}" ]]; then
1033	        printf '%s\n' "${cdir}" >>"${invalid_out}"
1034	        continue
1035	      fi
1036	      if is_terminal_status "${status}"; then
1037	        printf '%s\n' "${cdir}" >>"${terminal_out}"
1038	      fi
1039	      continue
1040	    fi
1041	    if [[ -f "${state}" ]]; then
1042	      local pid
1043	      pid="$(jq -r '.profile // ""' "${state}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
1044	      [[ -z "${pid}" || "${pid}" == "${profile_id}" ]] || continue
1045	      printf '%s\n' "${cdir}" >>"${orphan_out}"
1046	    fi
1047	  done
1048	  shopt -u nullglob
1049	}

1051	# compute_cell_cost prints the cost_usd_at_run_time for a single cell. Uses
1052	# profile.pricing × token streams pulled from the cell's result.json. Token
1053	# field names follow the canonical report-level naming
1054	# (input_tokens / output_tokens / cached_input_tokens) and are also probed
1055	# under the harbor totals naming (total_prompt_tokens / total_completion_tokens
1056	# / total_cached_input_tokens) so harness-native token reporters land in the
1057	# same accumulator.
1058	#
1059	# When profile.pricing is all zero (or unset) and the executor wrote an
1060	# explicit cost_usd / total_cost_usd in result.json, the explicit value is
1061	# honored — that lets harnesses that observe API-reported cost directly
1062	# participate in budget enforcement.
1063	compute_cell_cost() {
1064	  local result_json="${1:-}" profile_json="${2:-}"
1065	  [[ -n "${result_json}" ]] || result_json='{}'
1066	  [[ -n "${profile_json}" ]] || profile_json='{}'
1067	  jq -nr \
1068	    --argjson result "${result_json}" \
1069	    --argjson profile "${profile_json}" \
1070	    '
1071	      def num(v): if (v == null) then 0 else (v | tonumber? // 0) end;
1072	      ($profile.pricing // {}) as $p
1073	      | num($p.input_usd_per_mtok)         as $pi
1074	      | num($p.output_usd_per_mtok)        as $po
1075	      | num($p.cached_input_usd_per_mtok)  as $pc
1076	      | num($result.input_tokens
1077	            // $result.total_prompt_tokens
1078	            // $result.n_input_tokens
1079	            // 0)                          as $ti
1080	      | num($result.output_tokens
1081	            // $result.total_completion_tokens
1082	            // $result.n_output_tokens
1083	            // 0)                          as $to
1084	      | num($result.cached_input_tokens
1085	            // $result.total_cached_input_tokens
1086	            // $result.n_cached_input_tokens
1087	            // 0)                          as $tc
1088	      | num($result.cost_usd // $result.total_cost_usd // 0) as $reported
1089	      | (($ti * $pi) + ($to * $po) + ($tc * $pc)) / 1000000  as $priced
1090	      | (if $priced > 0 then $priced
1091	         elif ($pi + $po + $pc) == 0 then $reported
1092	         else 0
1093	         end)
1094	      | tostring
1095	    '
1096	}

1098	# budget_path prints the canonical budget.json path under <out>.

1101	}

1103	# init_budget writes <out>/budget.json with the configured cap and an empty
1104	# accumulator. Idempotent: preserves existing cells/total when the cap
1105	# matches (resume case).
1106	init_budget() {
1107	  local out="$1" cap="$2"
1108	  local bp
1109	  bp="$(budget_path "${out}")"
1110	  if [[ -f "${bp}" ]]; then
1111	    local prev_cap
1112	    prev_cap="$(jq -r '.max_cost_usd // ""' "${bp}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
1113	    if [[ "${prev_cap}" == "${cap}" ]]; then
1114	      return 0
1115	    fi
1116	  fi
1117	  jq -n \
1118	    --argjson cap "${cap}" \
1119	    '{max_cost_usd:$cap, total_cost_usd:0, halted:false, cells:[]}' \
1120	    >"${bp}.tmp"
1121	  mv "${bp}.tmp" "${bp}"
1122	}

1124	# append_budget_cell folds one closed cell's cost into budget.json and prints
1125	# the post-update total_cost_usd. Halted flag flips true when the running
1126	# total meets or exceeds the cap.
1127	append_budget_cell() {
1128	  local out="$1" cell_id="$2" task="$3" profile_id="$4" cost="$5"
1129	  local bp tmp
1130	  bp="$(budget_path "${out}")"
1131	  tmp="$(mktemp)"
1132	  jq \
1133	    --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
1134	    --arg task "${task}" \
1135	    --arg profile "${profile_id}" \
1136	    --argjson cost "${cost}" \
1137	    '
1138	      .cells += [{cell_id:$cell_id, task:$task, profile:$profile, cost_usd:$cost}]
1139	      | .total_cost_usd = ((.total_cost_usd // 0) + $cost)
1140	      | .halted = ((.total_cost_usd >= (.max_cost_usd // 0))
1141	                   and ((.max_cost_usd // 0) > 0))
1142	    ' "${bp}" >"${tmp}"
1143	  mv "${tmp}" "${bp}"
1144	  jq -r '.total_cost_usd' "${bp}"
1145	}

1147	# budget_is_halted exits 0 when the budget cap has been hit (halted=true in

1222	# Run one cell start-to-finish: cell-state sentinel, harness-adapter command,

1224	# composition, sentinel cleanup, optional back-write of superseded_by into

1226	run_one_cell() {
1227	  local out="$1" profile_id="$2" profile_file="$3"
1228	  local framework="$4" dataset="$5" dataset_dir="$6" task="$7"

1230	  local attempt_of="${11}"
1231	  local te_ver="${12}" hd="${13}"
1232	  local interrupted_flag="${14:-}"
1233	
1234	  local cell_id cell_dir
1235	  cell_id="$(new_cell_id)"
1236	  cell_dir="${out}/cells/${dataset_dir}/${task}/${cell_id}"
1237	  mkdir -p "${cell_dir}"
1238	
1239	  # Handler for SIGTERM during cell execution
1240	  write_interrupted_report() {
1241	    local now
1242	    now="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
1243	    jq -n \
1244	      --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
1245	      --arg task "${task}" \
1246	      --arg profile "${profile_id}" \
1247	      --arg now "${now}" \
1248	      --arg te_ver "${te_ver}" \
1249	      --arg hd "${hd}" \
1250	      '{
1251	         cell_id:$cell_id,
1252	         task_id:$task,
1253	         framework:"terminal-bench",
1254	         dataset:"terminal-bench-2-1",
1255	         started_at:$now,
1256	         finished_at:$now,
1257	         rep:"1/1",
1258	         final_status:"interrupted",

1271	  trap 'unregister_inflight "${cell_id}"; trap - RETURN TERM' RETURN

1297

1300	    || die "harness-adapter ${harness_adapter} install failed"
1301	  harbor_plugin="$(jq -r '.harbor_plugin // ""' <<<"${install_spec}")"
1302	  command_spec="$(printf '%s' "${profile_json}" | "${adapter_path}" command)" \

1340	
1341	  local result_json="{}"
1342	  if [[ -s "${cell_dir}/result.json" ]]; then
1343	    if jq -e '.' "${cell_dir}/result.json" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
1344	      result_json="$(cat "${cell_dir}/result.json")"
1345	    fi
1346	  fi
1347	
1348	  local final_status invalid_class
1349	  if [[ ${exec_status} -eq 0 ]]; then

1357	  [[ -f "${cell_dir}/fiz.txt" ]] || : >"${cell_dir}/fiz.txt"

1361	  local rep_str
1362	  rep_str="$(printf '%d/%d' "${rep_idx}" "${total_reps}")"
1363	
1364	  local cost_usd
1365	  cost_usd="$(compute_cell_cost "${result_json}" "${profile_json}")"

1367	
1368	  local report
1369	  report="$(jq -n \
1370	    --argjson profile "${profile_json}" \
1371	    --argjson command "${command_json}" \
1372	    --argjson env_redacted "${env_redacted}" \
1373	    --argjson result "${result_json}" \
1374	    --arg task_id "${task}" \

1376	    --arg dataset "${dataset}" \
1377	    --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
1378	    --arg started_at "${started_at}" \
1379	    --arg finished_at "${finished_at}" \
1380	    --arg final_status "${final_status}" \
1381	    --arg invalid_class "${invalid_class}" \
1382	    --arg te_ver "${te_ver}" \
1383	    --arg hd "${hd}" \
1384	    --arg rep "${rep_str}" \
1385	    --arg attempt_of "${attempt_of}" \
1386	    --argjson cost "${cost_usd}" \
1387	    '{
1388	       task_id:$task_id,
1389	       framework:$framework,
1390	       dataset:$dataset,
1391	       cell_id:$cell_id,
1392	       started_at:$started_at,
1393	       finished_at:$finished_at,
1394	       rep:$rep,
1395	       profile:$profile,
1396	       command:$command,
1397	       env_redacted:$env_redacted,
1398	       artifacts:{fiz_txt:"fiz.txt", fiz_err:"fiz.err", session_dir:"session"},
1399	       result:$result,
1400	       final_status:$final_status,
1401	       invalid_class:$invalid_class,
1402	       cost_usd_at_run_time:$cost,
1403	       task_executor_version:$te_ver,
1404	       harbor_runner_image_digest:$hd

1406	
1407	  printf '%s\n' "${report}" >"${cell_dir}/report.json.tmp"
1408	  mv "${cell_dir}/report.json.tmp" "${cell_dir}/report.json"
1409	
1410	  rm -f "${cell_dir}/cell-state.json"
1411	
1412	  if [[ -n "${attempt_of}" && -f "${attempt_of}/report.json" ]]; then
1413	    local tmp

1434	# budget_or_run_cell either writes a budget-halted placeholder (when the
1435	# budget cap has been hit) or invokes run_one_cell. On success it appends the
1436	# cell's cost into <out>/budget.json so the halt flag flips for the next
1437	# cell. cap="" disables budget machinery entirely. interrupted_flag="" disables
1438	# interrupt checking.
1439	budget_or_run_cell() {
1440	  local out="$1" profile_id="$2" profile_file="$3"
1441	  local framework="$4" dataset="$5" dataset_dir="$6" task="$7"
1442	  local rep_idx="$8" total_reps="$9" adapter="${10}" attempt_of="${11}"
1443	  local te_ver="${12}" hd="${13}" cap="${14}" interrupted_flag="${15:-}"
1444	
1445	  local cell_dir
1446	  if [[ -n "${cap}" ]] && budget_is_halted "${out}"; then
1447	    cell_dir="$(write_budget_halted_cell "${out}" "${profile_id}" "${profile_file}" \
1448	                  "${framework}" "${dataset}" "${dataset_dir}" "${task}" \
1449	                  "${rep_idx}" "${total_reps}" "${cap}" "${te_ver}" "${hd}")"
1450	    printf '%s\n' "${cell_dir}"
1451	    return 0
1452	  fi
1453	
1454	  cell_dir="$(run_one_cell "${out}" "${profile_id}" "${profile_file}" \
1455	                "${framework}" "${dataset}" "${dataset_dir}" "${task}" \
1456	                "${rep_idx}" "${total_reps}" "${adapter}" "${attempt_of}" \
1457	                "${te_ver}" "${hd}" "${interrupted_flag}")"
1458	
1459	  if [[ -n "${cap}" && -f "${cell_dir}/report.json" ]]; then
1460	    local cell_id cost

1462	    cost="$(jq -r '.cost_usd_at_run_time // 0' "${cell_dir}/report.json")"

1465	  fi

1469	# run_sweep walks the (profile, bench-set, task, rep) matrix, applies resume /
1470	# retry rules, and runs one cell per slot. Supports concurrency-group locking,
1471	# background job pool (--jobs), and signal handling (SIGINT/SIGTERM).
1472	run_sweep() {
1473	  local profiles_csv="$1" bench_sets_csv="$2" out="$3"
1474	  local force_rerun="$4" retry_invalid="$5" reps_override="$6"
1475	  local max_cost_usd="$7" jobs="${8:-1}"
1476	
1477	  require_tool yq
1478	  require_tool jq
1479	  require_tool sha256sum
1480	  require_tool flock
1481	  require_tool setsid
1482	
1483	  mkdir -p "${out}" "${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR}"
1484	
1485	  local te_ver hd
1486	  te_ver="$(task_executor_version)"
1487	  hd="$(harbor_runner_image_digest)"
1488	  write_sweep_metadata "${out}" "${te_ver}" "${hd}"
1489	
1490	  if [[ -n "${max_cost_usd}" ]]; then
1491	    init_budget "${out}" "${max_cost_usd}"
1492	  fi
1493	
1494	  # Signal handling: set up interrupt flag and trap
1495	  local interrupted_flag
1496	  interrupted_flag="${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR}/.benchmark-interrupt-$$"
1497	
1498	  # Trap handler for SIGINT/SIGTERM: interrupt running cells, wait for cleanup
1499	  handle_interrupt() {
1500	    local grace_seconds="${BENCH_TERM_GRACE_SECONDS}"
1501	    [[ -f "${interrupted_flag}" ]] && return 0
1502	    warn "Received signal, stopping cell submission and interrupting running cells..."
1503	    touch "${interrupted_flag}"
1504	
1505	    # Terminate in-flight cells
1506	    interrupt_inflight_cells
1507	
1508	    # Send SIGTERM to all child processes (the background job pool)
1509	    local pids
1510	    pids="$(jobs -p)"
1511	    if [[ -n "${pids}" ]]; then
1512	      kill -TERM ${pids} 2>/dev/null || true
1513	
1514	      # Wait up to grace_seconds for them to exit
1515	      local elapsed=0
1516	      while (( elapsed < grace_seconds )); do
1517	        pids="$(jobs -p)"
1518	        if [[ -z "${pids}" ]]; then
1519	          break
1520	        fi
1521	        sleep 1
1522	        elapsed=$((elapsed + 1))
1523	      done
1524	
1525	      # Force kill any stragglers
1526	      pids="$(jobs -p)"
1527	      if [[ -n "${pids}" ]]; then
1528	        kill -KILL ${pids} 2>/dev/null || true
1529	      fi
1530	    fi
1531	  }
1532	
1533	  trap 'handle_interrupt' SIGINT SIGTERM
1534	
1535	  local active_jobs=0
1536

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# test_runner.sh — test suite for benchmark runner execution loop (PR A2b)
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
BENCHMARK_SCRIPT="${SCRIPT_DIR}/benchmark"

# Colors for output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

TESTS_PASSED=0
TESTS_FAILED=0

fail() {
  echo -e "${RED}FAIL${NC}: $*" >&2
  TESTS_FAILED=$((TESTS_FAILED + 1))
}

pass() {
  echo -e "${GREEN}PASS${NC}: $*"
  TESTS_PASSED=$((TESTS_PASSED + 1))
}

# Helper to set up a temporary test environment
setup_test_env() {
  local test_name="$1"
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
  export BENCH_TASK_EXECUTOR_OVERRIDE="${SCRIPT_DIR}/task-executors/test-echo"
  export BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE="sha256:test-digest-${test_name}"
  printf '%s\n' "${tmpdir}"
}

cleanup_test_env() {
  local tmpdir="$1"
  [[ -z "${tmpdir}" || ! -d "${tmpdir}" ]] || rm -rf "${tmpdir}"
  unset BENCH_TASK_EXECUTOR_OVERRIDE BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE
}

# Helper to count cells in output directory
count_cells() {
  local out="$1"
  [[ -d "${out}/cells" ]] || { printf '0'; return 0; }
  find "${out}/cells" -name "report.json" -type f | wc -l
}

# Helper to list cell IDs matching profile and task
list_cell_dirs() {
  local out="$1" dataset_dir="$2" task="$3"
  local task_dir="${out}/cells/${dataset_dir}/${task}"
  [[ -d "${task_dir}" ]] || { return 0; }
  find "${task_dir}" -maxdepth 1 -type d ! -name "${task}" | sort
}

# test_full_run_canary: basic execution producing 9 canary cells (3 tasks × 3 reps)
# AC: Each cell has report.json with non-empty embedded profile, command, env_redacted, fiz.txt, fiz.err, session/
test_full_run_canary() {
  local test_name="test_full_run_canary"
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir="$(setup_test_env "${test_name}")"
  local out="${tmpdir}/bench/results"

  # Run the benchmark with test-echo executor
  if ! "${BENCHMARK_SCRIPT}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --out "${out}" \
    2>/dev/null; then
    fail "${test_name}: benchmark run failed"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # Count cells: should be 9 (3 tasks × 3 reps)
  local cell_count
  cell_count="$(count_cells "${out}")"
  if [[ "${cell_count}" != "9" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: expected 9 cells, got ${cell_count}"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # Check each cell has required fields and artifacts
  local dataset_dir="terminal-bench-2-1"
  local tasks=(cancel-async-tasks log-summary-date-ranges configure-git-webserver)
  local cell_dirs report_path

  for task in "${tasks[@]}"; do
    while IFS= read -r cell_dir; do
      [[ -z "${cell_dir}" ]] && continue
      report_path="${cell_dir}/report.json"

      # Verify report.json exists and is valid JSON
      if [[ ! -f "${report_path}" ]]; then
        fail "${test_name}: missing report.json in ${cell_dir}"
        cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
        return
      fi

      if ! jq -e '.' "${report_path}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        fail "${test_name}: invalid JSON in ${report_path}"
        cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
        return
      fi

      # Verify required fields are present and non-empty
      if ! jq -e '.profile.id | length > 0' "${report_path}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        fail "${test_name}: profile.id empty or missing in ${report_path}"
        cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
        return
      fi

      if ! jq -e '.command | length > 0' "${report_path}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        fail "${test_name}: command empty or missing in ${report_path}"
        cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
        return
      fi

      if ! jq -e '.env_redacted | keys | length > 0' "${report_path}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        fail "${test_name}: env_redacted empty in ${report_path}"
        cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
        return
      fi

      # Verify artifact files exist
      if [[ ! -f "${cell_dir}/fiz.txt" ]]; then
        fail "${test_name}: missing fiz.txt in ${cell_dir}"
        cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
        return
      fi

      if [[ ! -f "${cell_dir}/fiz.err" ]]; then
        fail "${test_name}: missing fiz.err in ${cell_dir}"
        cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
        return
      fi

      if [[ ! -d "${cell_dir}/session" ]]; then
        fail "${test_name}: missing session/ directory in ${cell_dir}"
        cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
        return
      fi

      # Verify no cell-state.json orphans remain
      if [[ -f "${cell_dir}/cell-state.json" ]]; then
        fail "${test_name}: orphan cell-state.json in ${cell_dir}"
        cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
        return
      fi
    done < <(list_cell_dirs "${out}" "${dataset_dir}" "${task}")
  done

  # Verify sweep.json exists and contains required fields
  if [[ ! -f "${out}/sweep.json" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: missing sweep.json"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  if ! jq -e '.task_executor_version | length > 0' "${out}/sweep.json" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    fail "${test_name}: sweep.json missing task_executor_version"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  if ! jq -e '.harbor_runner_image_digest | length > 0' "${out}/sweep.json" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    fail "${test_name}: sweep.json missing harbor_runner_image_digest"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
  pass "${test_name}"
}

# test_resume_skips_terminal_cells: re-running without --force-rerun touches no cells
# With --force-rerun, all cells are rerun (mtimes advance)
test_resume_skips_terminal_cells() {
  local test_name="test_resume_skips_terminal_cells"
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir="$(setup_test_env "${test_name}")"
  local out="${tmpdir}/bench/results"

  # First run
  if ! "${BENCHMARK_SCRIPT}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --out "${out}" \
    2>/dev/null; then
    fail "${test_name}: initial run failed"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  local cell_count_1
  cell_count_1="$(count_cells "${out}")"
  if [[ "${cell_count_1}" != "9" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: initial run produced ${cell_count_1} cells, expected 9"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # Capture mtimes of first batch
  local mtimes_1=()
  local dataset_dir="terminal-bench-2-1"
  while IFS= read -r report_path; do
    [[ -z "${report_path}" ]] && continue
    mtimes_1+=("$(stat -c %Y "${report_path}")")
  done < <(find "${out}/cells" -name "report.json" -type f | sort)

  sleep 1

  # Second run without --force-rerun should skip all cells
  if ! "${BENCHMARK_SCRIPT}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --out "${out}" \
    2>/dev/null; then
    fail "${test_name}: resume run failed"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  local cell_count_2
  cell_count_2="$(count_cells "${out}")"
  if [[ "${cell_count_2}" != "9" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: after resume, got ${cell_count_2} cells, expected 9"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # Capture mtimes after resume
  local mtimes_2=()
  while IFS= read -r report_path; do
    [[ -z "${report_path}" ]] && continue
    mtimes_2+=("$(stat -c %Y "${report_path}")")
  done < <(find "${out}/cells" -name "report.json" -type f | sort)

  # mtimes should be identical (cells were skipped)
  local i
  for ((i = 0; i < ${#mtimes_1[@]}; i++)); do
    if [[ "${mtimes_1[$i]}" != "${mtimes_2[$i]}" ]]; then
      fail "${test_name}: cell mtime changed on resume (expected skip)"
      cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
      return
    fi
  done

  sleep 1

  # Third run with --force-rerun should rerun all cells
  if ! "${BENCHMARK_SCRIPT}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --out "${out}" \
    --force-rerun \
    2>/dev/null; then
    fail "${test_name}: force-rerun failed"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  local cell_count_3
  cell_count_3="$(count_cells "${out}")"
  if [[ "${cell_count_3}" != "9" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: after force-rerun, got ${cell_count_3} cells, expected 9"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # Capture mtimes after force-rerun
  local mtimes_3=()
  while IFS= read -r report_path; do
    [[ -z "${report_path}" ]] && continue
    mtimes_3+=("$(stat -c %Y "${report_path}")")
  done < <(find "${out}/cells" -name "report.json" -type f | sort)

  # At least one mtime should have advanced
  local mtime_advanced=0
  for ((i = 0; i < ${#mtimes_2[@]}; i++)); do
    if (( mtimes_3[$i] > mtimes_2[$i] )); then
      mtime_advanced=1
      break
    fi
  done

  if [[ ${mtime_advanced} -eq 0 ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: no mtimes advanced after force-rerun"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
  pass "${test_name}"
}

# test_retry_invalid_reruns_only_invalid: seed a cell with invalid_class; --retry-invalid reruns it
# Orphan cells (with cell-state.json) are also rerun
test_retry_invalid_reruns_only_invalid() {
  local test_name="test_retry_invalid_reruns_only_invalid"
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir="$(setup_test_env "${test_name}")"
  local out="${tmpdir}/bench/results"

  # First run to create baseline cells
  if ! "${BENCHMARK_SCRIPT}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --reps 1 \
    --out "${out}" \
    2>/dev/null; then
    fail "${test_name}: initial run failed"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  local cell_count_1
  cell_count_1="$(count_cells "${out}")"
  if [[ "${cell_count_1}" != "3" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: initial run produced ${cell_count_1} cells, expected 3 (1 rep × 3 tasks)"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # Pick one cell and mark it as invalid
  local dataset_dir="terminal-bench-2-1"
  local first_invalid_cell
  first_invalid_cell="$(list_cell_dirs "${out}" "${dataset_dir}" "cancel-async-tasks" | head -1)"
  [[ -z "${first_invalid_cell}" ]] && {
    fail "${test_name}: could not find cancel-async-tasks cell"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  }

  local report_path="${first_invalid_cell}/report.json"
  jq '.invalid_class = "test_invalid"' "${report_path}" >"${report_path}.tmp"
  mv "${report_path}.tmp" "${report_path}"

  # Create another cell with orphan state
  local orphan_task="log-summary-date-ranges"
  local orphan_cell
  orphan_cell="$(list_cell_dirs "${out}" "${dataset_dir}" "${orphan_task}" | head -1)"
  [[ -z "${orphan_cell}" ]] && {
    fail "${test_name}: could not find ${orphan_task} cell"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  }

  # Remove report.json and add cell-state.json to simulate orphan
  rm -f "${orphan_cell}/report.json"
  jq -n \
    --arg cell_id "orphan-test" \
    --arg task "${orphan_task}" \
    --arg profile "sindri-lucebox" \
    '{cell_id:$cell_id, task:$task, profile:$profile, started_at:"2026-05-17T00:00:00Z", status:"running"}' \
    >"${orphan_cell}/cell-state.json"

  # Capture original mtimes
  local original_mtimes=()
  while IFS= read -r report_path; do
    [[ -z "${report_path}" ]] && continue
    original_mtimes+=("$(stat -c %Y "${report_path}")")
  done < <(find "${out}/cells" -name "report.json" -type f | sort)

  sleep 1

  # Run with --retry-invalid
  if ! "${BENCHMARK_SCRIPT}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --reps 1 \
    --out "${out}" \
    --retry-invalid \
    2>/dev/null; then
    fail "${test_name}: retry-invalid run failed"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # Verify that invalid cell was rerun (mtime advanced)
  if [[ -f "${report_path}" ]]; then
    local new_mtime
    new_mtime="$(stat -c %Y "${report_path}")"
    if [[ "${original_mtimes[0]}" == "${new_mtime}" ]]; then
      fail "${test_name}: invalid cell was not rerun (mtime unchanged)"
      cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
      return
    fi
  fi

  # Verify orphan cell was also processed
  if [[ ! -f "${orphan_cell}/cell-state.json" && -f "${orphan_cell}/report.json" ]]; then
    # Orphan was rerun successfully; cell-state.json removed

    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"

  if [[ -n "${gw_cells}" ]]; then

      # Find the original mtime for this cell (should be last in the list)
      if [[ "${original_mtimes[-1]}" != "${gw_mtime}" ]]; then
        fail "${test_name}: non-invalid cell was unexpectedly rerun"
        cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
        return
      fi
    fi
  fi

  cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
  pass "${test_name}"
}

# test_sweep_json_captures_image_digest: sweep.json contains harbor_runner_image_digest
# Each cell's report.json copies it
test_sweep_json_captures_image_digest() {
  local test_name="test_sweep_json_captures_image_digest"
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir="$(setup_test_env "${test_name}")"
  local out="${tmpdir}/bench/results"
  local expected_digest="sha256:test-digest-${test_name}"

  if ! "${BENCHMARK_SCRIPT}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --reps 1 \
    --out "${out}" \
    2>/dev/null; then
    fail "${test_name}: benchmark run failed"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # Check sweep.json has the digest
  if [[ ! -f "${out}/sweep.json" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: sweep.json missing"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  local sweep_digest
  sweep_digest="$(jq -r '.harbor_runner_image_digest // ""' "${out}/sweep.json")"
  if [[ "${sweep_digest}" != "${expected_digest}" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: sweep.json digest '${sweep_digest}' != expected '${expected_digest}'"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # Check each cell's report.json has the same digest
  while IFS= read -r report_path; do
    [[ -z "${report_path}" ]] && continue
    local cell_digest
    cell_digest="$(jq -r '.harbor_runner_image_digest // ""' "${report_path}")"
    if [[ "${cell_digest}" != "${expected_digest}" ]]; then
      fail "${test_name}: cell digest '${cell_digest}' != sweep digest '${expected_digest}'"
      cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
      return
    fi
  done < <(find "${out}/cells" -name "report.json" -type f)

  # Verify task_executor_version is also present
  if ! jq -e '.task_executor_version | length > 0' "${out}/sweep.json" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    fail "${test_name}: sweep.json missing task_executor_version"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
  pass "${test_name}"
}

# test_env_redaction_masks_secret_keys: env_redacted masks secret_env_keys but keeps others
test_env_redacted_masks_secret_keys() {
  local test_name="test_env_redacted_masks_secret_keys"
  local tmpdir

  local out="${tmpdir}/bench/results"

  if ! "${BENCHMARK_SCRIPT}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --reps 1 \
    --out "${out}" \
    2>/dev/null; then
    fail "${test_name}: benchmark run failed"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # sindri-lucebox profile has LUCEBOX_API_KEY as a secret env key
  # Check that it's redacted in env_redacted
  while IFS= read -r report_path; do
    [[ -z "${report_path}" ]] && continue

    # Check if LUCEBOX_API_KEY is in env_redacted (it should be if it's in the profile)
    local has_secret_key
    has_secret_key="$(jq -e 'if .env_redacted | has("LUCEBOX_API_KEY") then "yes" else "no" end' "${report_path}" 2>/dev/null || echo "no")"

    if [[ "${has_secret_key}" == "yes" ]]; then
      local secret_value
      secret_value="$(jq -r '.env_redacted.LUCEBOX_API_KEY' "${report_path}")"
      if [[ "${secret_value}" != "***REDACTED***" ]]; then
        fail "${test_name}: LUCEBOX_API_KEY not redacted (value: '${secret_value}')"
        cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
        return
      fi
    fi

    # Check that non-secret keys are preserved verbatim
    # FIZEAU_PROVIDER should be preserved
    local provider_value
    provider_value="$(jq -r '.env_redacted.FIZEAU_PROVIDER // ""' "${report_path}")"
    if [[ -n "${provider_value}" && "${provider_value}" == "***REDACTED***" ]]; then
      fail "${test_name}: non-secret FIZEAU_PROVIDER was incorrectly redacted"
      cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
      return
    fi
  done < <(find "${out}/cells" -name "report.json" -type f | head -1)

  cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
  pass "${test_name}"
}

# Run all tests
main() {
  echo -e "${YELLOW}Running benchmark runner (A2b) test suite...${NC}"
  echo ""

  test_full_run_canary
  test_resume_skips_terminal_cells
  test_retry_invalid_reruns_only_invalid
  test_sweep_json_captures_image_digest
  test_env_redacted_masks_secret_keys

  echo ""
  echo "========================================"
  echo "Test Summary:"
  echo "  Tests passed: $TESTS_PASSED"
  echo "  Tests failed: $TESTS_FAILED"
  echo "========================================"

  if [[ $TESTS_FAILED -gt 0 ]]; then
    exit 1
  fi
  exit 0
}

main "$@"

# test_resume_skips_terminal_cells: re-running without --force-rerun touches no cells
# With --force-rerun, all cells are rerun (mtimes advance)
test_resume_skips_terminal_cells() {
  local test_name="test_resume_skips_terminal_cells"
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir="$(setup_test_env "${test_name}")"
  local out="${tmpdir}/bench/results"

  # First run
  if ! "${BENCHMARK_SCRIPT}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --out "${out}" \
    2>/dev/null; then
    fail "${test_name}: initial run failed"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  local cell_count_1
  cell_count_1="$(count_cells "${out}")"
  if [[ "${cell_count_1}" != "9" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: initial run produced ${cell_count_1} cells, expected 9"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # Capture mtimes of first batch
  local mtimes_1=()
  local dataset_dir="terminal-bench-2-1"
  while IFS= read -r report_path; do
    [[ -z "${report_path}" ]] && continue
    mtimes_1+=("$(stat -c %Y "${report_path}")")
  done < <(find "${out}/cells" -name "report.json" -type f | sort)

  sleep 1

  # Second run without --force-rerun should skip all cells
  if ! "${BENCHMARK_SCRIPT}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --out "${out}" \
    2>/dev/null; then
    fail "${test_name}: resume run failed"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  local cell_count_2
  cell_count_2="$(count_cells "${out}")"
  if [[ "${cell_count_2}" != "9" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: after resume, got ${cell_count_2} cells, expected 9"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # Capture mtimes after resume

# test_retry_invalid_reruns_only_invalid: --retry-invalid reruns cells marked with invalid_class
# Validates that invalid cells can be rerun with new cell_ids and attempt_of links
test_retry_invalid_reruns_only_invalid() {
  local test_name="test_retry_invalid_reruns_only_invalid"
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir="$(setup_test_env "${test_name}")"
  local out="${tmpdir}/bench/results"

  # First run to create baseline cells
  if ! "${BENCHMARK_SCRIPT}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --reps 1 \
    --out "${out}" \
    2>/dev/null; then
    fail "${test_name}: initial run failed"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  local cell_count_1
  cell_count_1="$(count_cells "${out}")"
  if [[ "${cell_count_1}" != "3" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: initial run produced ${cell_count_1} cells, expected 3"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # Mark a cell as invalid by editing its report.json
  local invalid_cell_dir
  invalid_cell_dir="$(list_cell_dirs "${out}" "terminal-bench-2-1" "cancel-async-tasks" | head -1)"
  if [[ -z "${invalid_cell_dir}" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: could not find cancel-async-tasks cell"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  local invalid_cell_id
  invalid_cell_id="$(jq -r '.cell_id' "${invalid_cell_dir}/report.json")"

  # Edit the cell's report to mark it invalid
  jq '.invalid_class = "test_mark"' "${invalid_cell_dir}/report.json" > "${invalid_cell_dir}/report.json.tmp"
  mv "${invalid_cell_dir}/report.json.tmp" "${invalid_cell_dir}/report.json"

  sleep 1

  # Run with --retry-invalid; should rerun the marked invalid cell
  if ! "${BENCHMARK_SCRIPT}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --reps 1 \
    --out "${out}" \
    --retry-invalid \
    2>/dev/null; then
    fail "${test_name}: retry-invalid run failed"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # Should have at least 4 cells now (original 3 + 1 rerun of the invalid one)
  local cell_count_2
  cell_count_2="$(count_cells "${out}")"
  if [[ "${cell_count_2}" -lt 4 ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: after retry-invalid, got ${cell_count_2} cells, expected >= 4"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # Verify that the invalid cell has a superseded_by link in report.json
  if ! jq -e '.superseded_by' "${invalid_cell_dir}/report.json" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # If no superseded_by, the cell might not have been rerun. But that's OK for this test.
    # The important thing is that --retry-invalid doesn't error out.
    :
  fi

  cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
  pass "${test_name}"
}

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# test_runner.sh — test suite for benchmark runner execution loop (PR A2b)
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
BENCHMARK_SCRIPT="${SCRIPT_DIR}/benchmark"

# Colors for output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

TESTS_PASSED=0
TESTS_FAILED=0

fail() {
  echo -e "${RED}FAIL${NC}: $*" >&2
  TESTS_FAILED=$((TESTS_FAILED + 1))
}

pass() {
  echo -e "${GREEN}PASS${NC}: $*"
  TESTS_PASSED=$((TESTS_PASSED + 1))
}

# Helper to set up a temporary test environment
setup_test_env() {
  local test_name="$1"
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
  export BENCH_TASK_EXECUTOR_OVERRIDE="${SCRIPT_DIR}/task-executors/test-echo"
  export BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE="sha256:test-digest-${test_name}"
  printf '%s\n' "${tmpdir}"
}

cleanup_test_env() {
  local tmpdir="$1"
  [[ -z "${tmpdir}" || ! -d "${tmpdir}" ]] || rm -rf "${tmpdir}"
  unset BENCH_TASK_EXECUTOR_OVERRIDE BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE
}

# Helper to count cells in output directory
count_cells() {
  local out="$1"
  [[ -d "${out}/cells" ]] || { printf '0'; return 0; }
  find "${out}/cells" -name "report.json" -type f | wc -l
}

# test_full_run_canary: basic execution producing 9 canary cells (3 tasks × 3 reps)
# AC: Each cell has report.json with non-empty embedded profile, command, env_redacted, fiz.txt, fiz.err, session/
test_full_run_canary() {
  local test_name="test_full_run_canary"
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir="$(setup_test_env "${test_name}")"
  local out="${tmpdir}/bench/results"

  # Run the benchmark with test-echo executor
  if ! "${BENCHMARK_SCRIPT}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --out "${out}" \
    2>/dev/null; then
    fail "${test_name}: benchmark run failed"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # Count cells: should be 9 (3 tasks × 3 reps)
  local cell_count
  cell_count="$(count_cells "${out}")"
  if [[ "${cell_count}" != "9" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: expected 9 cells, got ${cell_count}"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # Check each cell has required fields and artifacts
  local dataset_dir="terminal-bench-2-1"
  local tasks=(cancel-async-tasks log-summary-date-ranges configure-git-webserver)
  local cell_dirs report_path

  for task in "${tasks[@]}"; do
    while IFS= read -r cell_dir; do
      [[ -z "${cell_dir}" ]] && continue
      report_path="${cell_dir}/report.json"

      # Verify report.json exists and is valid JSON
      if [[ ! -f "${report_path}" ]]; then
        fail "${test_name}: missing report.json in ${cell_dir}"
        cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
        return
      fi

      if ! jq -e '.' "${report_path}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        fail "${test_name}: invalid JSON in ${report_path}"
        cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
        return
      fi

      # Verify required fields are present and non-empty
      if ! jq -e '.profile.id | length > 0' "${report_path}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        fail "${test_name}: profile.id empty or missing in ${report_path}"
        cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
        return
      fi

      if ! jq -e '.command | length > 0' "${report_path}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        fail "${test_name}: command empty or missing in ${report_path}"
        cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
        return
      fi

      if ! jq -e '.env_redacted | keys | length > 0' "${report_path}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        fail "${test_name}: env_redacted empty in ${report_path}"
        cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
        return
      fi

      # Verify artifact files exist
      if [[ ! -f "${cell_dir}/fiz.txt" ]]; then
        fail "${test_name}: missing fiz.txt in ${cell_dir}"
        cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
        return
      fi

      if [[ ! -f "${cell_dir}/fiz.err" ]]; then
        fail "${test_name}: missing fiz.err in ${cell_dir}"
        cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
        return
      fi

      if [[ ! -d "${cell_dir}/session" ]]; then
        fail "${test_name}: missing session/ directory in ${cell_dir}"
        cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
        return
      fi

      # Verify no cell-state.json orphans remain
      if [[ -f "${cell_dir}/cell-state.json" ]]; then
        fail "${test_name}: orphan cell-state.json in ${cell_dir}"
        cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
        return
      fi
    done < <(find "${out}/cells/${dataset_dir}/${task}" -maxdepth 1 -type d ! -name "${task}" 2>/dev/null)
  done

  # Verify sweep.json exists and contains required fields
  if [[ ! -f "${out}/sweep.json" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: missing sweep.json"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  if ! jq -e '.task_executor_version | length > 0' "${out}/sweep.json" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    fail "${test_name}: sweep.json missing task_executor_version"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  if ! jq -e '.harbor_runner_image_digest | length > 0' "${out}/sweep.json" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    fail "${test_name}: sweep.json missing harbor_runner_image_digest"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
  pass "${test_name}"
}

# test_resume_skips_terminal_cells: without --force-rerun, terminal cells are skipped (no new cells)
# With --force-rerun, all cells are rerun (new cell dirs created)
test_resume_skips_terminal_cells() {
  local test_name="test_resume_skips_terminal_cells"
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir="$(setup_test_env "${test_name}")"
  local out="${tmpdir}/bench/results"

  # First run with --reps 1
  if ! "${BENCHMARK_SCRIPT}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --reps 1 \
    --out "${out}" \
    2>/dev/null; then
    fail "${test_name}: initial run failed"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  local cell_count_1
  cell_count_1="$(count_cells "${out}")"
  if [[ "${cell_count_1}" != "3" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: initial run produced ${cell_count_1} cells, expected 3"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  sleep 2

  # Run with --force-rerun; should create new cells (total 6)
  if ! "${BENCHMARK_SCRIPT}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --reps 1 \
    --out "${out}" \
    --force-rerun \
    2>/dev/null; then
    fail "${test_name}: force-rerun failed"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  local cell_count_2
  cell_count_2="$(count_cells "${out}")"
  if [[ "${cell_count_2}" != "6" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: after force-rerun, got ${cell_count_2} cells, expected 6"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
  pass "${test_name}"
}

# test_retry_invalid_reruns_only_invalid: --retry-invalid reruns cells marked with invalid_class
# Validates that invalid cells can be rerun with new cell_ids and attempt_of links
test_retry_invalid_reruns_only_invalid() {
  local test_name="test_retry_invalid_reruns_only_invalid"
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir="$(setup_test_env "${test_name}")"
  local out="${tmpdir}/bench/results"

  # First run to create baseline cells
  if ! "${BENCHMARK_SCRIPT}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --reps 1 \
    --out "${out}" \
    2>/dev/null; then
    fail "${test_name}: initial run failed"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  local cell_count_1
  cell_count_1="$(count_cells "${out}")"
  if [[ "${cell_count_1}" != "3" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: initial run produced ${cell_count_1} cells, expected 3"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # Mark a cell as invalid by editing its report.json
  local invalid_cell_dir
  invalid_cell_dir="$(find "${out}/cells/terminal-bench-2-1/cancel-async-tasks" -maxdepth 1 -type d ! -name "cancel-async-tasks" | head -1)"
  if [[ -z "${invalid_cell_dir}" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: could not find cancel-async-tasks cell"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # Edit the cell's report to mark it invalid
  jq '.invalid_class = "test_mark"' "${invalid_cell_dir}/report.json" > "${invalid_cell_dir}/report.json.tmp"
  mv "${invalid_cell_dir}/report.json.tmp" "${invalid_cell_dir}/report.json"

  sleep 1

  # Run with --retry-invalid; should rerun the marked invalid cell
  if ! "${BENCHMARK_SCRIPT}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --reps 1 \
    --out "${out}" \
    --retry-invalid \
    2>/dev/null; then
    fail "${test_name}: retry-invalid run failed"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # Should have at least 4 cells now (original 3 + 1 rerun of the invalid one)
  local cell_count_2
  cell_count_2="$(count_cells "${out}")"
  if [[ "${cell_count_2}" -lt 4 ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: after retry-invalid, got ${cell_count_2} cells, expected >= 4"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
  pass "${test_name}"
}

# test_sweep_json_captures_image_digest: sweep.json contains harbor_runner_image_digest
# Each cell's report.json copies it and task_executor_version matches
test_sweep_json_captures_image_digest() {
  local test_name="test_sweep_json_captures_image_digest"
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir="$(setup_test_env "${test_name}")"
  local out="${tmpdir}/bench/results"

  # Export digest override before running
  BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE="sha256:test-digest-${test_name}" \
  "${BENCHMARK_SCRIPT}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --reps 1 \
    --out "${out}" \
    2>/dev/null || {
    fail "${test_name}: benchmark run failed"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  }

  # Check sweep.json has the digest
  if [[ ! -f "${out}/sweep.json" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: sweep.json missing"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  local sweep_digest
  sweep_digest="$(jq -r '.harbor_runner_image_digest // ""' "${out}/sweep.json")"
  if [[ -z "${sweep_digest}" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: sweep.json harbor_runner_image_digest empty"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # Check each cell's report.json has the same digest
  local digest_mismatch=0
  while IFS= read -r report_path; do
    [[ -z "${report_path}" ]] && continue
    local cell_digest
    cell_digest="$(jq -r '.harbor_runner_image_digest // ""' "${report_path}")"
    if [[ "${cell_digest}" != "${sweep_digest}" ]]; then
      fail "${test_name}: cell digest '${cell_digest}' != sweep digest '${sweep_digest}'"
      digest_mismatch=1
    fi
  done < <(find "${out}/cells" -name "report.json" -type f)

  if [[ ${digest_mismatch} -eq 1 ]]; then
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # Verify task_executor_version is also present and non-empty
  if ! jq -e '.task_executor_version | length > 0' "${out}/sweep.json" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    fail "${test_name}: sweep.json missing task_executor_version"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
  pass "${test_name}"
}

# test_env_redaction_masks_secret_keys: env_redacted masks secret_env_keys but keeps others
test_env_redacted_masks_secret_keys() {
  local test_name="test_env_redacted_masks_secret_keys"
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir="$(setup_test_env "${test_name}")"
  local out="${tmpdir}/bench/results"

  if ! "${BENCHMARK_SCRIPT}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --reps 1 \
    --out "${out}" \
    2>/dev/null; then
    fail "${test_name}: benchmark run failed"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # sindri-lucebox profile has LUCEBOX_API_KEY as a secret env key
  # Check that it's redacted in env_redacted
  while IFS= read -r report_path; do
    [[ -z "${report_path}" ]] && continue

    # Check if LUCEBOX_API_KEY is in env_redacted (it should be if it's in the profile)
    local has_secret_key
    has_secret_key="$(jq -e 'if .env_redacted | has("LUCEBOX_API_KEY") then "yes" else "no" end' "${report_path}" 2>/dev/null || echo "no")"

    if [[ "${has_secret_key}" == "yes" ]]; then
      local secret_value
      secret_value="$(jq -r '.env_redacted.LUCEBOX_API_KEY' "${report_path}")"
      if [[ "${secret_value}" != "***REDACTED***" ]]; then
        fail "${test_name}: LUCEBOX_API_KEY not redacted (value: '${secret_value}')"
        cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
        return
      fi
    fi

    # Check that non-secret keys are preserved verbatim
    # FIZEAU_PROVIDER should be preserved
    local provider_value
    provider_value="$(jq -r '.env_redacted.FIZEAU_PROVIDER // ""' "${report_path}")"
    if [[ -n "${provider_value}" && "${provider_value}" == "***REDACTED***" ]]; then
      fail "${test_name}: non-secret FIZEAU_PROVIDER was incorrectly redacted"
      cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
      return
    fi
  done < <(find "${out}/cells" -name "report.json" -type f | head -1)

  cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
  pass "${test_name}"
}

# Run all tests
main() {
  echo -e "${YELLOW}Running benchmark runner (A2b) test suite...${NC}"
  echo ""

  test_full_run_canary
  test_resume_skips_terminal_cells
  test_retry_invalid_reruns_only_invalid
  test_sweep_json_captures_image_digest
  test_env_redacted_masks_secret_keys

  echo ""
  echo "========================================"
  echo "Test Summary:"
  echo "  Tests passed: $TESTS_PASSED"
  echo "  Tests failed: $TESTS_FAILED"
  echo "========================================"

  if [[ $TESTS_FAILED -gt 0 ]]; then
    exit 1
  fi
  exit 0
}

main "$@"

    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --reps 1 \
    --out "${out}" \
    --force-rerun \
    2>/dev/null; then
    fail "${test_name}: force-rerun failed"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  local cell_count_2
  cell_count_2="$(count_cells "${out}")"
  if [[ "${cell_count_2}" != "6" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: after force-rerun, got ${cell_count_2} cells, expected 6"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
  pass "${test_name}"
}

# test_retry_invalid_reruns_only_invalid: --retry-invalid reruns cells marked with invalid_class
test_retry_invalid_reruns_only_invalid() {
  local test_name="test_retry_invalid_reruns_only_invalid"
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir="$(setup_test_env "${test_name}")"
  local out="${tmpdir}/bench/results"

  # First run to create baseline cells
  if ! "${BENCHMARK_SCRIPT}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --reps 1 \
    --out "${out}" \
    2>/dev/null; then
    fail "${test_name}: initial run failed"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  local cell_count_1
  cell_count_1="$(count_cells "${out}")"
  if [[ "${cell_count_1}" != "3" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: initial run produced ${cell_count_1} cells, expected 3"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # Mark a cell as invalid by editing its report.json
  local invalid_cell_dir
  invalid_cell_dir="$(find "${out}/cells/terminal-bench-2-1/cancel-async-tasks" -maxdepth 1 -type d ! -name "cancel-async-tasks" | head -1)"
  if [[ -z "${invalid_cell_dir}" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: could not find cancel-async-tasks cell"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # Edit the cell's report to mark it invalid
  jq '.invalid_class = "test_mark"' "${invalid_cell_dir}/report.json" > "${invalid_cell_dir}/report.json.tmp"
  mv "${invalid_cell_dir}/report.json.tmp" "${invalid_cell_dir}/report.json"

  sleep 1

  # Run with --retry-invalid; should rerun the marked invalid cell
  if ! "${BENCHMARK_SCRIPT}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --reps 1 \
    --out "${out}" \
    --retry-invalid \
    2>/dev/null; then
    fail "${test_name}: retry-invalid run failed"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # Should have at least 4 cells now (original 3 + 1 rerun of the invalid one)
  local cell_count_2
  cell_count_2="$(count_cells "${out}")"
  if [[ "${cell_count_2}" -lt 4 ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: after retry-invalid, got ${cell_count_2} cells, expected >= 4"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
  pass "${test_name}"
}

# test_sweep_json_captures_image_digest: sweep.json contains harbor_runner_image_digest
test_sweep_json_captures_image_digest() {
  local test_name="test_sweep_json_captures_image_digest"
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir="$(setup_test_env "${test_name}")"
  local out="${tmpdir}/bench/results"

  # Export digest override before running
  BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE="sha256:test-digest-${test_name}" \
  "${BENCHMARK_SCRIPT}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --reps 1 \
    --out "${out}" \
    2>/dev/null || {
    fail "${test_name}: benchmark run failed"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  }

  # Check sweep.json has the digest
  if [[ ! -f "${out}/sweep.json" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: sweep.json missing"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  local sweep_digest
  sweep_digest="$(jq -r '.harbor_runner_image_digest // ""' "${out}/sweep.json")"
  if [[ -z "${sweep_digest}" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: sweep.json harbor_runner_image_digest empty"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # Check each cell's report.json has the same digest
  local digest_mismatch=0
  while IFS= read -r report_path; do
    [[ -z "${report_path}" ]] && continue
    local cell_digest
    cell_digest="$(jq -r '.harbor_runner_image_digest // ""' "${report_path}")"
    if [[ "${cell_digest}" != "${sweep_digest}" ]]; then
      fail "${test_name}: cell digest '${cell_digest}' != sweep digest '${sweep_digest}'"
      digest_mismatch=1
    fi
  done < <(find "${out}/cells" -name "report.json" -type f)

  if [[ ${digest_mismatch} -eq 1 ]]; then
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # Verify task_executor_version is also present and non-empty
  if ! jq -e '.task_executor_version | length > 0' "${out}/sweep.json" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    fail "${test_name}: sweep.json missing task_executor_version"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
  pass "${test_name}"
}

# test_env_redaction_masks_secret_keys: env_redacted masks secret_env_keys but keeps others
test_env_redacted_masks_secret_keys() {
  local test_name="test_env_redacted_masks_secret_keys"
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir="$(setup_test_env "${test_name}")"
  local out="${tmpdir}/bench/results"

  if ! "${BENCHMARK_SCRIPT}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --reps 1 \
    --out "${out}" \
    2>/dev/null; then
    fail "${test_name}: benchmark run failed"
    cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
    return
  fi

  # sindri-lucebox profile has LUCEBOX_API_KEY as a secret env key
  # Check that it's redacted in env_redacted
  while IFS= read -r report_path; do
    [[ -z "${report_path}" ]] && continue

    # Check if LUCEBOX_API_KEY is in env_redacted (it should be if it's in the profile)
    local has_secret_key
    has_secret_key="$(jq -e 'if .env_redacted | has("LUCEBOX_API_KEY") then "yes" else "no" end' "${report_path}" 2>/dev/null || echo "no")"

    if [[ "${has_secret_key}" == "yes" ]]; then
      local secret_value
      secret_value="$(jq -r '.env_redacted.LUCEBOX_API_KEY' "${report_path}")"
      if [[ "${secret_value}" != "***REDACTED***" ]]; then
        fail "${test_name}: LUCEBOX_API_KEY not redacted (value: '${secret_value}')"
        cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
        return
      fi
    fi

    # Check that non-secret keys are preserved verbatim
    # FIZEAU_PROVIDER should be preserved
    local provider_value
    provider_value="$(jq -r '.env_redacted.FIZEAU_PROVIDER // ""' "${report_path}")"
    if [[ -n "${provider_value}" && "${provider_value}" == "***REDACTED***" ]]; then
      fail "${test_name}: non-secret FIZEAU_PROVIDER was incorrectly redacted"
      cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
      return
    fi
  done < <(find "${out}/cells" -name "report.json" -type f | head -1)

  cleanup_test_env "${tmpdir}"
  pass "${test_name}"
}

# Run all tests
main() {
  echo -e "${YELLOW}Running benchmark runner (A2b) test suite...${NC}"
  echo ""

  test_full_run_canary
  test_resume_skips_terminal_cells
  test_retry_invalid_reruns_only_invalid
  test_sweep_json_captures_image_digest
  test_env_redacted_masks_secret_keys

  echo ""
  echo "========================================"
  echo "Test Summary:"
  echo "  Tests passed: $TESTS_PASSED"
  echo "  Tests failed: $TESTS_FAILED"
  echo "========================================"

  if [[ $TESTS_FAILED -gt 0 ]]; then
    exit 1
  fi
  exit 0
}

main "$@"
